2606.19998.title: Tri-Info: Generalizable, Interpretable Failure Prediction for VLA Models via Information Theory
2606.19998.paper_summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are increasingly deployed across diverse tasks, yet they remain black boxes whose physical interactions can cause irreversible harm, making generalizable and interpretable failure detection essential. We observe that successful and failed rollouts carry systematically different information-theoretic signatures. Building on this, we formalize VLA control as a closed-loop information pipeline and derive the Triple Information-theoretic (Tri-Info) signals that capture whether actions remain diverse, temporally consistent, and coupled to state transitions. Across six VLA models and three benchmark environments, Tri-Info matches the strongest baselines in-domain. Moreover, Tri-Info transfers across architectures, environments, and the sim-to-real gap without retraining, reaching 83\% accuracy on real-world tasks where prior detectors collapse to chance. This establishes Tri-Info as a simple yet powerful method that not only detects failures with strong cross-domain generalization, but also delivers interpretable diagnostics of the underlying failure modes.
2606.19998.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言-行動（VLA）模型越來越多地應用於各種任務，但它們仍然是黑箱，其物理互動可能造成不可逆的傷害，因此可泛化和可解釋的故障檢測變得至關重要。我們觀察到成功和失敗的展開在信息理論簽名上系統性地不同。在此基礎上，我們將VLA控制形式化為一個閉環信息管道，並推導出三重信息理論（Tri-Info）信號，以捕捉行動是否保持多樣性、時間一致性以及與狀態轉換的耦合。在六個VLA模型和三個基準環境中，Tri-Info在領域內匹配最強的基準。此外，Tri-Info在不同架構、環境和模擬到現實的差距中無需重新訓練即可轉移，並在現實世界任務中達到83\%的準確率，而先前的檢測器則崩潰至隨機水平。這確立了Tri-Info作為一種簡單而強大的方法，不僅能夠以強大的跨域泛化檢測故障，還能提供對潛在故障模式的可解釋診斷。
2606.19998.author: Jinghan Yang et.al.
2606.19998.authors: Jinghan Yang, Yunchao Zhang, Wang Yuan, Haolun Wan, Jiaming Zhang, Zhengyang Hu, Yanchao Yang
